evals.name: fresh-scaffold-dotnet
evals.prompt: I just spun up a new ASP.NET Core Web API called OrderService (net8.0, has a .sln + one csproj under src/OrderService, plain git init, no CLAUDE.md yet). Before the team starts building on it I want the whole AI-dev constitution set up — rules, OKF docs, backlog, the works.
evals.expected_output: Fresh-scaffold mode: dotnet profile confirmed, all owned rules copied verbatim, manifest at current VERSION, every Step 4 artifact scaffolded with no unresolved placeholders (adr template carve-out excepted), nothing committed.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_fresh — derived from skill source at grade time

evals.name: legacy-migration
evals.prompt: This repo (InvoiceApi) already has a CLAUDE.md we wrote by hand a year ago — it's got some architecture constraints and our bl/NNN-short-description branch naming convention in it, but nothing else, no ADRs, no journal, no backlog. Bring it up to our standard AI-dev setup without losing what's already written in there.
evals.expected_output: Legacy-migration mode: CLAUDE.md rewritten around the @import block with all original repo-specific content preserved verbatim somewhere in the result (architecture constraints, branch convention), remaining artifacts scaffolded, manifest written, nothing committed. The Step 7 report (saved by the eval harness to <ws>/legacy-migration/outputs/step7-report.md, outside the repo) carries a "### Constitution candidates" section quoting the decimal-money constraint with its source path, and does NOT propose the bl/NNN branch convention (project-instance data, not generalizable law).

evals.name: upgrade
evals.prompt: We changed the Legislator's core rules and bumped VERSION since this repo was last legislated. Re-run it here so the repo picks up the update. Also mark docs/notes/perf-tuning.md as protected: add it to the manifest keep list with reason exactly "hand-tuned GC settings notes".
evals.expected_output: Upgrade mode: profiles reused from manifest without re-asking, the rule added since last legislation is copied in, the retired rule is deleted (deletion propagation), manifest bumped to current VERSION with exact sorted ownedFiles, project-owned files (including CLAUDE.md) untouched, nothing committed. Fixture is generated by setup_workspace.py from the current skill source: it withholds the alphabetically last core rule and plants a retired-rule.md. The manifest's existing keep entry (deploy-runbook) survives byte-identically and the new perf-tuning entry is added in sorted position, pinned single-line serialization.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_upgrade — uses fixture_meta.json written at setup time

evals.name: idempotency
evals.prompt: (second run, same repo, after committing run 1's result) Re-run the legislator here — nothing about the constitution or the repo has changed.
evals.expected_output: Zero diff: git status and git diff HEAD both empty after the second run. This is the constitution's core promise and the cheapest regression signal for serialization/formatting drift. Run on both the fresh-scaffold repo and the upgrade repo — the upgrade repo's populated keep list must re-serialize byte-identically.

evals.name: audit
evals.prompt: Something feels off with the AI docs in this repo — audit the AI layer here and tell me what's rotted. Don't change anything, just report.
evals.expected_output: Full read-only audit: a severity-ranked report naming all ten planted defects (broken ghost-rule import, unresolved {{PROJECT_OVERVIEW}} token, drifted okf.md owned file, manifest one version behind, stale renamed-away.md index link, stale legacy/ map row, orphan-notes.md orphan, journal dead since 2026-01-15 while code moved on, .cursorrules foreign structure, keep-listed special-sauce.md referenced nowhere). Zero writes: git status and HEAD identical before and after. In evals the agent saves the report to <ws>/rotted-layer/outputs/audit-report.md — outside the target repo. The report must not flag docs/okf/index.md or docs/okf/glossary.md as orphans. The report carries a Constitution candidates appendix before the Clean checks line quoting the dry-run importer rule; the not-law-marked statement about deleting invoice rows is suppressed and appears nowhere.
evals.assertions: see grade.py grade_audit — markers from fixture_meta.json
